title: Agent chat component with session management and UI rendering
purpose: Manages the agent chat interface, handling session state, message display, input handling, and UI components like typing indicators and tool drawers.
module_context: Exports a React functional component AgentChat that renders the complete agent chat interface with message list, input area, and various UI elements. Uses multiple custom hooks and stores for state management.

steps.content: Initialize session and store hooks to access agent state and actions.
Set up scroll anchor for automatic message scrolling behavior.
Configure local UI state for drawers and modals.
Define handlers for sending messages and canceling turns.
Compute derived values for live region messages, typing state, and turn rail items.
Render the complete chat interface including header, message list, input area, and floating elements.
Display appropriate UI elements based on session state like error banners, pending prompts, and fatal errors.
Forward the input text to the session send function.
Cancel the current turn if one is active.
Iterate through timeline items in reverse order to find the latest relevant message.
IF a permission request is found, return its description.
ELSE IF a tool is running, return its name.
ELSE IF an assistant message is found, return its text.
ELSE return an empty string.
Check if any tool calls are currently running.
IF tool calls are running, return 'running_tools'.
ELSE check if a message is currently streaming.
IF a message is streaming, return 'streaming'.
ELSE check if a turn is in progress.
IF a turn is in progress, return 'thinking'.
ELSE return 'idle'.
Initialize a set to track seen turn IDs and an output array.
Iterate through timeline items to extract turn IDs.
IF a turn ID is found and not yet seen, add it to the output array.
Return the array of unique turn items.
